description_long: This is a vintage-style lantern with a robust, cylindrical metal body and a wide, circular base for stability. The lantern features a central glass enclosure, likely intended to protect the flame or light source, surrounded by a crisscrossed metal wire guard for added durability and safety. The top section includes a vented cap and a looped handle, allowing for easy carrying or hanging. The metal surfaces are heavily weathered, showing signs of rust and age, which gives the lantern a rugged, utilitarian appearance. The design suggests it is an oil lamp, commonly used in outdoor or rustic urban settings. There are no visible decorative elements, emphasizing function over ornamentation.
description: A weathered, metal oil lantern with a cylindrical glass enclosure and protective wire guard, featuring a looped handle and a wide, rusted base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full glass enclosure, crisscrossed wire guard, and the central body of the lantern.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the metal supports and the profile of the handle and vented cap.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the wire guard and glass enclosure visible, but less detail on the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the side profile of the lantern and the curvature of the base and handle.
